results.title: Best customer self-service portals for 2026
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > 3. [HubSpot Service Hub](https://www.hubspot.com/products/service)
Content:
Chatbots
Search engine
Omnichannel messaging
Customer surveys |
HubSpot Service Hub offers support features like live chat, help desks, and ticketing. The knowledge base can store answers to your customers’ most common questions and organize and tag them so they’re discoverable. Chatbots can share those articles, and conversation intelligence tools reveal insights into what the bots do right and what needs work. Along with its customer self-service features, HubSpot Service Hub also integrates with other HubSpot platforms like Marketing, Sales, and Content.
 ... 
Section Title: The best customer self-service portals > ... > 4. [Intercom](https://www.intercom.com/)
Content:
Chatbots
Customizable
Product tours
Automated onboarding |
Intercom is a conversational relationship platform (CRP) that provides customer support through bots, personalized messaging, and chats. Intercom’s platform includes a conversational marketing feature, incorporating customizable online chatbots that engage website visitors in real-time conversations. The chatbots can pull answers from sources such as help center articles, public URLs, and PDFs. The software also offers onboarding features, including one-off messages and integrated campaigns, complete with in-product messaging and customizable banners for the top of your app or website.
 ...

Content:

[Image credit](https://www.liveagent.com/features/customer-portal/)

Section Title: ... > 9. [Salesforce Service Cloud](https://www.salesforce.com/service/cloud/)
Content:
Omnichannel routing
Knowledge management
Generative AI
Reporting and analytics |
Salesforce Service Cloud is a customer service management platform with self-service capabilities. Users can create articles on the interface or upload files from third-party sources like Google Docs. Generative AI search helps customers find answers by auto-generating suggestions from relevant articles. Service Cloud is also part of the wider Salesforce ecosystem, so Salesforce users can integrate their systems if they operate with the other platforms.

excerpts:
Knowledge Base
CRM
Records
Add and manage attachments on records and activities
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Available with any of the following [subscriptions](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/billing/w...
Content:
[All products and plans](https://www.hubspot.com/products/crm)
Additional subscriptions required for certain features
Upload and attach files to records and activities to keep context and resources accessible in HubSpot. Attachments help teams reference documents, images, or other files directly from records such as contacts, deals, and services. You can also manage existing attachments by renaming, filtering, or removing them as needed. ## Understand requirements Subscription required A *Professional* or *Enterprise* subscription is required to use team specific permissions . The permissions required to view attachments depend on how the attachments were added: * When attachments are added on a record , users must have Notes view permissions to view them. Notes view permissions options:
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Add or remove an attachment on a record To add an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to yo...
Content:
```
+ **Contacts:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Contacts**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-1) .
+ **Projects:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Projects**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-970/views/all) .
```
Section Title: ... > Add or remove an attachment on a record To add an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to yo...
Content:
Click the **name** of the record. In the right sidebar, scroll down to the *Attachments* card. Click **Add** , then select:
**Your computer** : upload a new file from your computer. **Existing file in HubSpot** : select an existing file in your HubSpot account. In the right panel, click the **name** of the file you want to attach. To remove an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
**Contacts:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Contacts**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-1) . **Projects:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Projects**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-970/views/all) . Click the **name** of the record. In the right sidebar, scroll down to the *Attachments* card. Click the **X** in the upper right of the attachment.
Section Title: ... > Add or remove an attachment on a record To add an attachment on a record: 1. Navigate to yo...
Content:
In the dialog box select from the following:
**Delete** : permanently delete the attachment on the record and the logged activity. This option appears for sent one-to-one emails or emails logged via the HubSpot sales extension . **Detach** : remove the attachment added to the record but keep it in the files tool . This option appears for attachments added via the files tool .
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
```
+ Contacts: In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Contacts**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-1) .
+ Projects: In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Projects**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-970/views/all) .
```
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
Click the **name** of the record. In the left sidebar, click the **icon** for the activity. In the activity editor, click the attach **attach icon** , then select **Upload new** to add a file from your computer, or **Choose existing** to select a file already added to HubSpot. Select the **file** , finish adding the activity's details, then click **Log [name of activity]** or **Create note** . To add an attachment to an existing activity:
Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
Contacts: In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Contacts**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-1) . Projects: In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Projects**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-970/views/all) . Click the **name** of the record. Click the **Activities** tab. Hover over an activity and click the **text box** .
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
In the text editor, click the attach **attach icon** , then select **Upload new** to add a file from your computer, or **Choose existing** to select a file already added to HubSpot. Select the **file** , then click **Save** . **Please note:** when you [send one-to-one emails](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/one-to-one-email/send-and-reply-to-one-to-one-emails) in HubSpot or log an email using HubSpot Sales , non-inline attached files are automatically added to the *Attachments* section of the record. Any inline attachments will not be added. ## Manage attachments Once you've added attachments, you can view all attached files on each record, filter them to locate the attachments you need, and rename or remove them. 1.
Section Title: ... > Add an attachment to an activity To add create an activity and add an attachment to it: 1. ...
Content:
Navigate to your records (e.g., contacts, projects):
**Contacts:** In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Contacts**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-1) . **Projects** : In your HubSpot account, navigate to [**CRM** > **Projects**](https://app.hubspot.com/l/contacts/objects/0-970/views/all) . Click the **name** of the record. In the right panel, scroll down to the *Attachments* section. If you have more than 5 attachments, click **Show more** to display 5 additional files. To view and manage the full list of attachments, **View all attachments** . In the right panel:
Enter **text** in the *Search name and file type* text field, to search for a file by name or type. Click **File type** , then select the **checkboxes** of types of files you want to view. Click **Source** , then select the **checkboxes** of sources you want to filter by.

results.title: The Ultimate HubSpot Integrations Guide (2026 Edition)
excerpts:
Section Title: The Ultimate HubSpot Integrations Guide (2026 Edition) > **Types of HubSpot Integrations**
Content:
| **Integration Type** | **Best For** | **Popular Platforms** |
| **eCommerce Integrations** | Online stores, B2C & B2B commerce | Shopify, WooCommerce, Magento, BigCommerce |
| **CMS Integrations** | Content websites, blogs, marketing sites | WordPress, Drupal, Joomla, Webflow |
| **Team Collaboration** | Communication, file sharing, project management | Slack, Google Drive, Microsoft Teams, Asana |
| **Sales & Support Tools** | Sales enablement, customer support | Salesforce, Zendesk, Intercom, Zoom |
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Google Drive Integration: Centralized Document Management**
Content:
Although HubSpot does not provide a native integration with Google Drive, you can use the two systems together through automation tools, such as Zapier, Make, or Integration Glue, to build strong document workflow.
**Automated Document Workflows**
Save HubSpot email attachments to designated Google Drive folders by contact or deal automatically.
Automatically create Google drive folders every time a deal or project is created in HubSpot.
Back-up contact files and contracts into Google Drive to be accessed by the team.
Proposals or contracts can be created in Google Docs using HubSpot information and appended to deals automatically.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Unified Customer Journey Orchestration**
Content:
Create cross platform customer experience which flows across platforms. It is possible that a prospect finds your brand by reading a post on WordPress, gets nurtured by HubSpot, chats with your team on the Slack-connected live chat and makes their purchase on your Shopify. To organize this journey, it is necessary to know how the data are shared across platforms and to develop workflows that carry the customers through the different stages of the journey.

results.title: CRM Best Practices for Architecture & Engineering Firms - Monograph
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > **What if our team already resists the tools we have? Why would CRM be different?**
Content:
Your team resists tools that create administrative burden without delivering value. When CRM lives in isolation from project management, every won opportunity requires manual data entry into multiple systems. That's not resistance. That's rational behavior.
Connect your CRM to project management so opportunity data flows automatically into project setup. Client requirements become project briefs. Budget discussions turn into phase budgets. Stakeholder contacts appear in project dashboards.
Your technical professionals will use systems that eliminate busywork and provide context they need. They'll resist systems that force them to hunt through three platforms to answer basic questions about client requirements.

results.title: 10 Best Omnichannel CRM Platforms: Features & Pricing ...
excerpts:
Section Title: description: The 10 best omnichannel CRM platforms: respond.io, Kustomer, Kommo, Salesforce, HubS...
Content:
**Modern Omnichannel CRMs for B2C & High-Volume Messaging:** These are best for high-volume B2C communication, focusing on integrating chat, calls, and the full customer lifecycle in a unified way. Best platforms: **Respond.io, Kommo, Trengo, Intercom.**
**B2B Sales-Led CRMs with Omnichannel Add-Ons:** These platforms are primarily built around traditional B2B sales pipelines, accounts, and complex sales cycles.Best platforms: **Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM.**
**Traditional Omnichannel Support Platforms:** These platforms are primarily focused on classic, structured support and service desk functions that are typically ticket-based.Best platforms: **Zendesk, Freshdesk, Kustomer.**
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Top 10 best omnichannel CRMs
Content:
| **Category** | **Primary Focus & Core Workflows** | **Platforms** | **Recommended Choice** |
| **Modern Omnichannel CRMs for B2C & High-Volume Messaging** | Focus on messaging, calls, & customer lifecycle in a unified way. These modern platforms are suitable for B2C and high volumes of communication. | Respond.io, Kommo, Trengo, Intercom | Respond.io |
| **B2B Sales-Led CRMs with Omnichannel Add-Ons** | Primarily B2B and sales-led. Workflows built around pipelines, accounts, and traditional sales cycles. | Salesforce, HubSpot, Zoho CRM | Salesforce |
| **Traditional Omnichannel Support Platforms** | Primarily focused on classic omnichannel support and service desk functions. | Zendesk, Freshdesk, Kustomer | Zendesk |
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Features
Content:
Channels: Major messaging apps (WhatsApp, Instagram, Telegram, etc.), Email and Telephony/SMS (via Twilio/RingCentral).
Integrations: Connects with Zapier, Make, Shopify, Zendesk, and Stripe; does not natively integrate with major CRMs like Salesforce/HubSpot.
Dashboards & Reports: Customizable dashboards with real-time widgets to track KPIs, lead distribution, message volume, and median reply time.
AI Capabilities: AI Agent handles engagement, qualification, FAQs (using uploaded data), and product recommendations. AI Copilot tools adjust tone, summarize, and suggest replies.
Workflow Automation: Visual builder (Salesbot) creates complex bots for greetings, data gathering, appointments, and payments. Digital Pipeline automates actions based on lead stage.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Features
Content:
Channels: Unifies all channels, including WhatsApp, Email, Live Chat, Voice/Telephony, SMS, Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and Telegram.
Integrations: Integrates with major external tools like Shopify, Zendesk, Salesforce, HubSpot and Zapier to sync data and enable actions.
Dashboards and Reports: Customizable dashboards for monitoring KPIs. Includes detailed reports on team performance, conversation volume and customer interactions.
AI Capabilities: AI Agent (HelpMate) provides automated responses and suggested replies to speed up agents. AI Journeys automate conversations and workflows across multiple channels. AI Actions allow the AI Agent to trigger external APIs (GET, POST, etc.) for tasks like checking order status.
Workflow Automation: Uses Rules and Automation for handling basic repetitive tasks (e.g., auto-replies, conversation routing) and advanced AI Journeys for complex, multi-step, multi-channel workflows.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Omnichannel CRM 4. Intercom — Conversational omnichannel CRM
Content:
![](https://assets2-proxy.respond.io/ph8r57dk1q9w/5tjY5zDyn4TSrKVFbsA5zk/8f1d4c4ee14903028f82ac8ad802c106/Intercom_inbox.png?fm=jpg&q=80&w=1200&fl=progressive)
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Customer sentiment and platform rating
Content:
Intercom is highly valued for its intuitive chat interface, strong engagement tools, seamless web/app integration and real-time user tracking with effective automation flows. However, customers frequently cite significant drawbacks related to cost and capability gaps, including high costs and pricing complexity when scaling, weaker analytics and less robust ticket-management features.
G2 rating: 4.5/5
Capterra rating: 4.5/5
Section Title: ... > Features
Content:
Channels: Intercom supports in-app chat, web chat, email, mobile push and messaging via connectors for social/IM apps such as WhatsApp and SMS.
Integrations: Intercom offers an app store with hundreds of apps and integrations, and connects with major systems such as CRMs, BI tools and Slack.
Dashboards and reports: The platform provides analytics and reporting features, including conversation analytics, user behavior tracking, team productivity dashboards and custom charting.
AI capabilities: Its “Fin” AI agent and other AI-driven features enable automated responses, conversation triage/triaging, agent assist, and workflow automation based on behavior and context.
Workflow automation: Intercom includes a visual workflow builder with triggers and rules (e.g., "user does X → send message/route to agent"), routing logic, bots + automation of routine tasks.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Features
Content:
Channels: Supports email, live chat, website chatbots, and integrates support channels into one shared inbox across marketing, sales and service.
Integrations: Built directly on the HubSpot CRM so data flows across marketing, sales and service; integrates with tools like Jira, Slack, and 1,900+ apps.
Dashboards and reports: Offers custom dashboards, reporting for ticket resolution, CSAT/NPS feedback and team performance analytics.
AI capabilities: Includes AI-powered ticket classification, routing, conversation summaries and knowledge base automation (HubSpot’s Breeze AI Agents).
Workflow automation: Robust automation features: ticket routing, follow-ups, status changes, cross-team notifications, automated feedback surveys.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Pricing
Content:
For Zoho CRM the “most popular” plan is the Enterprise edition. When billed on a monthly basis (no annual contract), it is priced at US $ 50 per user/month.
Potential add-on costs include extra file or data storage (e.g., 5 GB for around US $4/ 4/month), additional team or portal user licenses, premium 24/7 support, and extensions to increase feature limits, such as higher API or automation capacities.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Pros
Content:
Strong omnichannel support that consolidates customer interactions from many channels into one platform.
Scalable architecture suitable for high-volume support operations and enterprise needs.
Extensive third-party integrations and app ecosystem for customization.
Advanced automation features including routing, workflows and macros that boost agent efficiency.
